segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The junction chamber came apart in stages.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: First: the leftmost rib sheared. Not slowly. Not with warning. Just -- SNAP -- and tons of stone cascaded from the vault in wagon-sized blocks.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "LEFT SIDE!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex shouted as they scrambled right and the floor where they'd been standing vanished under rubble.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain's rhythm broke into a riot: THUD--thud--THUD -- thud--THUD, a crowd of heartbeats not their own. Caelin's pulse tried to follow and stumbled; a sick skip behind his sternum knocked his balance half a step off.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dust hissed from the ribs like breath through teeth. The air carried the wet-clay breath of new fractures and a metal tang, and static prickled along their forearms.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale in Caelin's arm blazed black-violet at the edges -- full corruption bloom, the Seal's influence flooding the chamber like invisible tide. Not close. Not here. But present. Aware. The obsidian plate pulsed irregular, excited: thud-THUD-thud-THUD.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It wants me, he understood. The Seal. It can FEEL the Ember. And it's using whatever power it has to collapse the mountain, to drop me closer to it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Second: the right-hand arch -- their intended exit -- let go like a builder's curse fulfilled. The pre-stressed keystone clicked, a clean crack ran the span, and the arch folded inward in a slate-gray avalanche that sealed the passage throat to floor.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Shadows slid ahead of the collapse -- thin whips testing the raw edge where ward-glyphs didn't quite reach. Caelin's scale flared red-gold; its halo seared those probes to curling soot that skittered away like burned spiders.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Third: the floor cracked. A black lightning zigzag split the chamber from waist to wall and widened with every breath. The Seal's poly-rhythm devolved into pure thrill -- THUD-thud-THUD-thud-THUD -- no pattern, just a chained thing thrashing in joy at the scent of fire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: ELOWEN was at the rear -- counting heads, keeping stragglers from becoming ghosts -- when the slab under her boots lowered, wrong as a breath drawn by stone. She saw Durgan sprint clear; Nyxara took the widening cut in one clean leap; Caelin turned back toward her --

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not enough time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She chose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She slammed her staff and called the new magic: not growth, load-sharing. Roots erupted from living wood, racing across the failing geometry in a glowing web -- but these weren't the sickly pale green of dying nature-magic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They blazed emerald shot through with threads of silver-gold, the Abjuration influence from the Deep Hall now woven into her very being.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The roots didn't brace the mountain; they borrowed weight -- took the load for three breaths, then two, then one -- as the transformed power coursed through her tattoos until the channels screamed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The smell of burning green knifed through dust -- sharp chlorophyll-sweet, life paying itself out, but underneath it the metallic tang of ward-magic being consumed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The web held. The lowering paused.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "GO!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her voice cut the riot clean.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can't hold -- MOVE!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's hand went up -- chalk sigil, instinct, geometry she still believed in -- and the ward simply wasn't there. The mountain swallowed the shape before it formed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She chose the only thing left: she hooked Caelin's harness with both hands and dragged him back before the slab surged. She chose too: one lost or many. Not a prayer. Just arithmetic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He wrenched to go anyway. The floor lurched.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's tattoos burned from emerald-and-silver to pure white. The root-web flared to ash, spent -- not shattered, not explosive, simply gone. The transformation magic that had given her new purpose consumed itself in one final act of protection.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The slab dropped. She went with it, swallowed in a grinding maw of stone. No cry. No light -- neither green nor gold. No staff -- wood splintered as the last charge emptied.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dust boiled and sealed the black mouth into a gray scar.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above, the riot in the rock pitched higher, delighted. Caelin lunged for the edge --

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "MOVE OR -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara started, a warning that became prophecy as their section lowered under their boots.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They fell.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: One heartbeat: solid stone. The next: nothing. Caelin tumbled through choking dust, the world a roll of jagged edges and red sparks. Instinct screamed heat the air -- stupid instinct. Hot wind doesn't hold weight.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He tucked, took the rock on shoulder and thigh, let pain be ballast. A debris slope caught him -- cruel, angled, still better than a pit -- and he shed momentum in skin and blood.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Parallel on the slide, Nyxara fell with the eerie grace of someone who'd made a career out of surviving bad ideas. Pact-power whispered -- no force, no shove -- simply convinced the falling stones she wasn't part of the story.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Angles and experience did the rest. She surfed the avalanche to a jutting ledge and cut speed in a low, bruising crouch.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They hit. Caelin sprawled, gasping; his right arm lit in a lattice of red pain as the scale jarred against bone. The filaments flared angry red, screaming, then slowly faded back toward coal-glow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above, stone sealed on stone.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "CAELIN!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's voice knifed through muffling tons.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "CAN YOU HEAR ME?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He coughed grit.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "ALIVE! WE'RE ALIVE!"

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "CAN'T REACH YOU! ROUTE'S SEALED!"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "WE'LL FIND ANOTHER WAY!"

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "SURFACE EXIT -- EAST FACE -- WE'LL WAIT!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thin as thread, then gone. Either distance, or the mountain decided they'd said enough.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin sat, cataloguing damage: bruises blooming, shoulder shrieking, the lattice aching but holding. He hadn't cast. Could have. Wanted to. Didn't. Small victory.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara tested joints -- grimace, then a shrug.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Sprained. Mobile. Ten out of ten would not recommend."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stood, staggered, found purpose.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Mark the wall. Let them know."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He burned Draconic into a clean slab with a tight ember thread: ALIVE. FOLLOWING HEAT. FIND US SURFACE EAST.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Beneath it, he added Thornik's party sigil -- three short strokes, one long. So the others would know it was them.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's glow dimmed from ember-red back to soft coal as the magic settled.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Somewhere beyond the sealed chokes and ruined ribs, the noise shifted. The riot dropped back to patience; the world's pressure exhaled.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not below, not above -- everywhere. Presence, not location. Influence bleeding through leagues of stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Time to move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, already limping toward the only path that wasn't trying to kill them yet.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Before the mountain changes its mind. Or the pets find a new seam."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stood there a moment longer than the task required.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He kept thinking of small things. The way she'd pressed two fingers to stone and said it listens back. The first time she eased the lattice-burn and he'd been too surprised to say thank you.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The vine-marks on her wrists the morning they left the Deep Hall -- two more blackened, and she hadn't mentioned it, just kept walking. He hadn't mentioned it either. He'd thought there was time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That was the part that would stay with him. Not the sound of the slab. The assumption of time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked once at the sealed scar. No token. No miracle. The stone didn't care, and he said it anyway:

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I'm sorry."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale warmed -- acknowledgment, not comfort.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He put her name away in the place where you put things you can't carry and can't put down. Then he turned into the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Up in the junction they'd abandoned, dust settled where green-and-gold light had been. No voice. No staff. No trace.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain's riot eased back to patient counting, as if the chain below had tested the links and found them holding -- for now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan stood at the edge of the sealed passage, ice-blue eyes tracking the crack where Caelin had disappeared. His shadow had leapt for that crack like a starving animal smelling meat -- reaching, stretching ten feet across rubble before he crushed it flat with will and pain.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The pressure burst something. Blood ran from both nostrils now, not a trickle but a stream. He wiped it with the back of his hand, stared at the red smear, then tucked his hand away.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Faster, he thought. It's happening faster.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one had seen. They were too busy staring at where their companions had fallen.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "We move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, voice hollow but functional.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Find another route. Reach the surface. Meet them at the east exit."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She didn't say if they're alive. The silence did it for her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik unrolled his journal with shaking hands.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Vent shaft route. Steep. Narrow. But it reaches surface."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He marked it with charcoal, the line wavering.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Two hours. Maybe three."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then we climb,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said. Her voice was flat, empty of its usual edge. She pressed a hand to her ribs, felt them creak, and kept it there. She turned to move -- and caught the tail end of Durgan tucking his hand into his sleeve. Not fast enough. The wrong colour on his upper lip.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She held the look for one breath. Filed it. Said nothing. Elowen was gone and Caelin was somewhere below and her ribs were broken and there was only so much room.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They turned toward the vent shaft entrance -- a crack in the chamber wall barely wide enough for shoulders.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the Seal counted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Patient. Waiting.
